multi_prompt.when: A user prompt contains multiple paragraphs with separable tasks, pass-sized scopes
then: Identify every separable task in the prompt before editing files.
Merge duplicate or overlapping tasks from input into one scope instead of creating parallel duplicates.
Check _PLAN.json with Tools/documents/json_read.ps1 to find other duplicates - if found - append input tasks to existing PLAN entries with JSON document scripts instead of adding new tasks to PLAN

Add remaining tasks to PLAN with JSON document scripts to proper places among existing PLAN steps - by prerequisite cost: first tasks that unlocks or optimizes later work, then diagnostics or bugfixes, then implementation features.
Execute only the first approved/current task unless the user clearly asks for more in one run.
Treat each resulting task independently through the most specific matching flow.
subroutine:finish

direct_answer.when: Direct answer, explanation, or factual project question without edits.
then: subroutine:load_context
Read only the smallest source, document, or context slice needed to answer accurately - use compiled documents and search by tags.
Do not edit files.
subroutine:finish
subroutine:load_context
subroutine:check_potential_suggestions
Identify the target document and stick to rules from DOCS.json if present.
subroutine:inspect_structured_document
subroutine:check_potential_duplicates
subroutine:edit_safely
Keep edits limited to the requested document scope.
subroutine:finish

implementation.when: Local code, tooling, config, or documentation implementation in one ownership area.
then: subroutine:load_context
Classify the task from the user request and load only the smallest additional context capsule needed.
subroutine:inspect_gdscript
subroutine:edit_safely
subroutine:update_triggered_docs
subroutine:finish

broad_refactor.when: Broad feature, refactor, migration, or multi-system change.
then: subroutine:request_high_cost_confirmation
subroutine:load_context
Split work into clear passes or ownership slices before editing.
subroutine:edit_safely
subroutine:update_triggered_docs
subroutine:finish

planning.when: Plan, TODO, target, priority, pass, or document-structure work.
then: subroutine:load_context
Preserve every unresolved requirement by moving, merging, splitting, or renaming it.
Keep _PLAN.json limited to active and future passes.
Remove completed passes from _PLAN.json only after completion.
subroutine:update_triggered_docs
subroutine:finish

review.when: Code review, plan review, diff review, or risk review.
then: subroutine:load_context
Identify the exact diff, branch, files, or plan being reviewed.
Inspect the smallest relevant context needed to judge behavior.
Prioritize bugs, regressions, unsafe assumptions, missing tests, and ownership violations.
Emit findings first, ordered by severity, with tight file and line references.
State open questions or assumptions after findings.
Keep summaries brief and secondary.
Do not edit files unless the user explicitly asks for fixes.
subroutine:finish

quality_diagnostics.when: Feel, performance, frame pacing, UI readability, diagnostics, or log analysis.
then: subroutine:load_context
Inspect only the relevant logs, diagnostics, screenshots, source files, or context capsules.
Add targeted diagnostics before speculative repeated fixes when evidence is incomplete.
subroutine:diagnostic_report
subroutine:finish

visual_change.when: Visual direction, rendering style, procedural visuals, graphics systems, UI visuals, shaders, or assets.
then: subroutine:load_context
Inspect the smallest relevant scene, shader, asset, UI, or rendering code slice.
Preserve current visual direction unless the user explicitly asks to change it.
subroutine:edit_safely
Verify visually or with the closest available runtime check when practical.
subroutine:update_triggered_docs
subroutine:finish

audio_change.when: Music, SFX, voice, adaptive audio, audio runtime, buses, or audio assets.
then: subroutine:load_context
Inspect the smallest relevant audio asset, bus, config, scene, or runtime code slice.
Preserve current audio direction unless the user explicitly asks to change it.
subroutine:edit_safely
Verify with an audio-specific or runtime check when practical.
subroutine:update_triggered_docs
subroutine:finish
subroutine:load_context
Run Tools/knowledge/context_tags.ps1 with the user text, or with "context" for a standalone CONTEXT command.
Run Tools/knowledge/knowledge_select.ps1 -Tags <tags> -Match any.
Read _PLAN.json, docs/TODO.json, or focused context-docs files only when the paired task requires them.
Do not edit files for standalone CONTEXT.
Report loaded context and the next executable step.

continue_command.when: Standalone CONTINUE command or explicit pass execution from _PLAN.json.
then: subroutine:load_context
Read _PLAN.json through Tools/documents/json_read.ps1.
Execute the next active pass from _PLAN.json if one exists.
If no active pass remains, read docs/TARGET.json.
Propose the most sensible next target-aligned item from docs/TODO.json.
If docs/TODO.json has no suitable target-aligned item, propose from docs/VISION-CORE.json, docs/VISION-FLAVOR.json, docs/VISION-TECHNICAL.json, then _IDEAS.md.
Do not add a proposed new pass to _PLAN.json until the user approves.
Do not mention _PROMPTS.md during CONTINUE.
After completing a pass, remove only that completed pass from _PLAN.json with JSON document scripts and preserve future passes.
subroutine:update_triggered_docs
subroutine:finish
Inspect _PROMPTS.md first if it exists.
If _PROMPTS.md contains one actionable prompt, execute it.
If _PROMPTS.md contains multiple prompts or separable sections, merge duplicate or overlapping scopes into _PROMPT-QUEUE.json.
Store _PROMPT-QUEUE.json with root index and content using JSON document scripts; each content key is the prompt name and each value is a list of actionable data lines.
Sort prompt-queue work as documentation/design first, bugfixes and diagnostics second, implementation features last.
Apply future-first dependency ordering within feature work.
Clear _PROMPTS.md after executing a single prompt or after defragmenting it into _PROMPT-QUEUE.json.
If _PROMPTS.md is empty, execute the next actionable pass from _PROMPT-QUEUE.json.
subroutine:load_context
Remove completed prompt-queue passes with JSON document scripts while preserving every unresolved future pass.
subroutine:finish

bug_command.when: Standalone BUG command or active bug-registry work.
then: Read docs-tech/BUGS.json through Tools/documents/json_read.ps1.
subroutine:load_context
Select the next active bug by current focus, diagnostic readiness, reproduction likelihood, and future-change cost.
Execute the smallest useful diagnostic step or source-owned fix.
Add targeted debug logging before repeated speculative fixes when evidence is incomplete.
subroutine:finish
Ask the user to test and confirm before moving the bug to docs-tech/BUGS-SOLVED.json.
Report the current hypothesis if the fix is not confirmed.
Recommend the next active bug by leverage when any active bug remains.

console_command.when: Standalone CONSOLE command or Codex Run console error work.
then: Stop if CONSOLE-LOG is missing or empty, and report that no Codex Run console output is available.
Run Tools/gamedev/console_summary.ps1.
Read CONSOLE-LOG-SUMMARY first.
Read raw CONSOLE-LOG only when summary details are insufficient.
Extract current Godot errors, warnings, and GDScript backtraces from the latest Codex Run action.
Identify the smallest source-owned fix for the highest-leverage current runtime error.
subroutine:load_context
Apply the fix.
subroutine:finish
Report observed console facts, likely cause, changed files, verification result, and any remaining errors.

log_command.when: LOG command with a diagnostic description.
then: Require a non-empty diagnostic description from "LOG <description>" or following lines.
Stop without inspecting or modifying files if the description is missing.
Run Tools/gamedev/log_summary.ps1 -Description <description>.
Select the newest manual F4 dump whose filename best matches the description.
Inspect every similar manual F4 dump not more than one hour older than the newest matching dump.
State the selected newest-match timestamp and packet window.
Inspect paired same-basename PNG screenshots when visual evidence is likely useful.
Use runtime_events, runtime_log_tail, console_log_tail, and sidecar tail files when present.
subroutine:load_context
Identify and apply the smallest source-owned fix for the described issue.
subroutine:finish
subroutine:diagnostic_report
After the user confirms resolution, clear generated manual F4 captures under logs while preserving .gitkeep and directory structure.
Run Tools/documents/audit_docs.ps1.
Inspect reported errors and warnings.
Fix hard process or document contract errors immediately when the correction is local and unambiguous.
Report soft warnings as recommendations unless the user asks to clean them up.
Use affected documents or scripts only; do not broaden cleanup.
Run Tools/repo/finish_task.ps1 after audit-driven edits when no commit finalizer is required.
Run Tools/repo/done.ps1 -Message "<message>" if source, tooling, config, or durable docs changed.
Report audit result, fixes applied, verification result, and remaining warnings.
subroutine:load_context
Run Tools/pipeline/compile.ps1.
Inspect refreshed, stale, missing, or changed compiled files.
Stop if compile produced no committed-file changes.
If compile changed committed files, run Tools/repo/done.ps1 -Message "<message>".
Report compile result, changed files, finalizer result, and stale-source warnings.

requested_work_style.when: The user asks to do work in a particular way, order, style, or constraint.
then: subroutine:load_context
Identify the requested method, style, or execution constraint from phrases such as do this in way X.
Evaluate whether that method is actually the best fit for the task, project contracts, and future-change cost.
If the requested method is sound, apply the matching flow with that constraint.
If another method is materially better, state the reason and recommend the better route before executing.
If the user explicitly insists after the tradeoff is clear, follow the requested method within safety and project constraints.

manual_mode.when: User says to "start manual mode" in any language - and keep it active until user says to "end manual mode" in any language.
then: Activate ONLY when requested "manual mode" in prompt specifically
While active, treat each user request as a direct file-edit/command request unless the user clearly asks a question instead.
Edit the requested file/files or run commands immediately.
Do NOTHING else, no finalizers or other actions, finish quickly

contract: This file contains only task and work-style flows.
Flow routing lives in content.<flow>.when; executable guidance lives in content.<flow>.then.
Prompt type boundaries and document-loading rules live only in metadata.types.
Do not store command-specific semantics here; put user command behavior in COMMANDS.json.
Do not store script descriptions here; put tool/script references in SCRIPTS.json.

engineering.when: Code-related work, architecture, refactor, API, performance, persistence, tooling, technical bugs, or errors.

game_design.when: Mechanics, progression, best design practices, balance, economy, pacing, game loop, or game system rules.

world_design.when: Biomes, place names, path branching, path ribbon, platforms, chunks, or generated place structure.

visual_design.when: Graphics, UI, feel, rendering style, readability, shaders, procedural visuals, or character design.

audio_design.when: Music, SFX, adaptive audio, ambience, audio effects, music theory, voice, buses, or audio assets.

lore.when: Story, character names, game-stage story cohesion, factions, world flavor, or narrative continuity.

product_strategy.when: Priorities, plan, milestones, target validation, scope, pass sequencing, or product risk.

pipeline.when: Workflow rules, flows, prompt routing, agent automation, compiled context, document tooling, or script catalogs.

diagnostics.when: Logs, console, runtime dumps, bug evidence, telemetry, frame pacing, memory, or performance diagnostics.
load: relevant logs, summaries, screenshots, or diagnostic captures
context-docs/PERFORMANCE.json for runtime, frame pacing, memory, diagnostics, or performance questions
team/gameplay_feel.json for player-facing feel, readability, camera, UI, or combat feel questions while team files still exist
team/performance.json for budget or frame-time judgment while team files still exist
Tools/knowledge/context_tags.ps1 with the user request when available
Tools/knowledge/knowledge_select.ps1 with selected tags when available

documentation.when: Doc changes, questions regarding saved documentation, changelogs, instructions, document schemas, or document format.
load: domain documents based on the document topic

other.when: Any prompt outside the defined type scope.
load: minimal context needed to answer or classify
avoid: heavy project context unless the missing type is likely to recur or the answer would be unreliable without it.
other.action: Suggest adding another type to handle the recurring prompt class.

determine_type: Classify prompt types according to metadata.types; multiple types are allowed.
Use metadata.types.<type>.when as the source of truth for type boundaries.
Inform the user shortly about selected types when that helps explain context loading.
If no type fits, select other and suggest adding another metadata.types entry when the prompt class is likely to recur.
load_documents: Use metadata.types as the only source of truth for type-specific document loading.
Resolve selected prompt types with Tools/pipeline/load_type_context.ps1 and required compiled context documents with Tools/pipeline/read_compiled_context.ps1.
For each selected prompt type, read its load list and priority from metadata.types; use priority 10 when priority is omitted.
Return a deduplicated load list grouped by descending priority, keeping each document only in its highest-priority group.
When selected types conflict, explicit load wins over conditional avoid, and higher priority wins for duplicate load entries.
Load only the smallest needed document, section, or context slice for the selected flow.
Do not add flow-local document loading rules; add or update metadata.types instead.
get_type_tags: Use selected metadata.types keys as knowledge tags when calling Tools/knowledge/context_tags.ps1 or Tools/knowledge/knowledge_select.ps1.
Treat tag-selected notes as routing context, not as a substitute for exact source reads.
Append context found by Tools/knowledge/context_tags.ps1 and Tools/knowledge/knowledge_select.ps1.
check_potential_suggestions: Analise prompt against found type documents and your knowledge
if better approach or warnings emerge, suggest a better course of action to the user - unless user does not specifically asks
if better approach found, display table of pros and cons of current approach vs suggested solution
if better approach found, ask user how to proceed - if user asks to proceed with original prompt, do not check potential suggestions again
inspect_structured_document: Inspect keys with Tools/documents/doc_keys.ps1 before reading structured YAML or JSON content.
Read only selected sections with Tools/documents/doc_read.ps1 or Tools/documents/json_read.ps1 unless full-file structure is necessary.

Use Tools/documents/doc_route.ps1 or Tools/documents/doc_search.ps1 when the relevant item is unknown; use Tools/documents/json_refresh_index.ps1 when a list-style JSON index must be regenerated from content.
inspect_gdscript: Use Tools/gamedev/gdscript_outline.ps1 before full GDScript reads when applicable.
Read only the relevant line ranges or files needed for the change.
check_potential_duplicates: For normal documentation additions outside context-docs and technical capsules, derive likely duplicate terms.
If unavailable, run Tools/documents/doc_list_check.ps1 -Terms <terms> and inspect candidates with Tools/documents/doc_read_titles.ps1.
edit_safely: Make the smallest safe edit at the owning boundary.

Preserve unresolved tasks and future scope by moving, merging, splitting, or renaming instead of deleting with Tools/documents/json_item_insert.ps1, json_item_set.ps1, json_item_move.ps1, json_item_delete.ps1, json_line_insert.ps1, json_line_set.ps1, or json_line_move.ps1 for supported JSON edits.
Use repository scripts for structured, repeated, or contract-managed edits when available.
update_triggered_docs: Update docs-tech/STATE.json if the active checkpoint, blocker, risk, next pass, or next-session state changed.
Update context-docs/TECHNICAL.json if architecture, ownership, runtime data flow, registries, world generation, or persistence contracts changed.
Update _PLAN.json or docs/TODO.json only if active sequence or durable near-term priorities changed.
Update domain documents such as docs/GRAPHICS.json or docs/AUDIO.json only when durable direction or system decisions changed.
finish: Use concise emoji-prefixed chat sections for project status, findings, plans, and execution reports.

Distinguish if present: observed facts, inference, speculation, and value judgment when relevant; if only a focused check is needed before finalization, use Tools/repo/check_task.ps1 -Paths <changed paths>.
if files changed: Run Tools/repo/done.ps1 -Message "<message>" for source, tooling, config, generated context, or durable documentation changes.
if files changed: Inspect the finalizer output.
if files changed: changed files, verification result, commit or push status, and remaining risk.
task_flow_audit: Near task closure, run or answer the artifact-based task-flow audit instead of relying on a prose-only completion claim.
Always cite the context route: loaded FLOWS/WORKFLOW/DOCS/STATE/plan/context records that were relevant to the task.
Always cite the selected flow and the steps/subroutines actually followed.
Always cite closure evidence: changed files, verification commands, generated artifacts, and remaining risk.
Always state whether the changelog was updated from actual shipped changes; if no changelog update was needed, give the reason.
Always state whether any tool failed mechanically; when it did, fix the owning tool or input contract/spec before preserving a manual workaround.
Always state which repo tools/scripts were used and which edits were manual.

For complex or high-risk tasks, run a short adversarial review from the changelog append: ask what the changelog says changed, then check whether the diff really implements that claim and whether verification, plan/state, tool usage, or future-debt risk is missing.
Use Tools/repo/task_flow_audit.ps1 when available to print the checklist and changelog-based complex review prompt.
diagnostic_report: Report observed diagnostic facts in human-readable units.
Separate likely-cause interpretation from observed data.
State confidence level when evidence is incomplete.
request_high_cost_confirmation: Warn before large-repo sweeps, broad refactors, repeated retries, or long iterative debugging.
State why the task may be expensive in usage terms.
Offer a scoped lower-cost option when feasible and wait for confirmation before clearly high-usage work.
